name: Valle del Rio Cidacos and ancient town of Turruncun — circular from Arnedillo
name_original: Valle del Río Cidacos y antigua pueblo de Turruncún — circular desde Arnedillo
region: Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa
region_intro: Road cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa offers diverse landscapes shaped by geological formations and the Cidacos River. The region features a mix of shrublands, pine and holm oak forests, and dramatic peaks like Peña Isasa and Peñalmonte. Cyclists can explore routes along the Cidacos Valley, which provides varied terrain from gentle river paths to more challenging climbs. This area, designated as a Zone of Special Conservation, presents a unique environment for no traffic road cycling routes. Best no traffic road cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Red Cliffs of Herce – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedo, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Cidacos Greenway, offering views of the Red Cliffs of Herce. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo, a moderate 26.9 miles (43.2 km) path. This route combines scenic stretches along the Cidacos Greenway with a visit to the thermal baths of Arnedillo. Local road cyclists also love the Arnedillo and thermal baths – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedo, a 34.9 miles (56.1 km) trail leading through the Cidacos Valley and past the Arnedillo thermal baths, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa is defined by the Cidacos Valley, diverse shrublands, and pine and holm oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides along the Greenway to more difficult ascents. The routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from 3 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no traffic road cycling routes are available in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: There are 3 dedicated no traffic road cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa, ranging from moderate to difficult difficulty levels. These routes primarily utilize the scenic Cidacos Greenway, offering a peaceful cycling experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on these no traffic road cycling routes?
A: The routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa offer varied terrain. You'll find gentle ascents and descents along the Cidacos Greenway, which follows a former railway line, making it relatively smooth. However, some routes also include climbs to higher elevations, providing diverse challenges and panoramic views of the Cidacos Valley and surrounding towns.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no traffic road cycling options?
A: While the listed routes are moderate to difficult, the &lt;strong&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; itself is an excellent option for families. As a former railway line, it offers a smooth path with gentle gradients, making it suitable for various cycling levels. You can explore sections of it, such as the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40017588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Cliffs of Herce – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 20 km loop.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the no traffic road cycling routes?
A: You can expect to encounter diverse natural beauty and historical sites. The routes often follow the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the Cidacos River. You might also pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy distant views of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its shrublands, pine forests, and unique yew forests like Tejeda de Peñalmonte.

Q: Are there any circular no traffic road bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, all the no traffic road cycling routes listed are circular. For example, you can enjoy the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 43 km, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40017714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and thermal baths – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, a 56 km difficult route.

Q: What is the best time of year for no traffic road cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer comfortable conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes without the intense heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Can I combine cycling with other activities, like visiting thermal baths?
A: Absolutely! The region is famous for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thermal Pools of Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;, where natural hot springs exceed 40ºC. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to include a visit to these relaxing baths, making for a perfect post-ride recovery.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter along these routes?
A: The region is a Zone of Special Conservation and a Biosphere Reserve, rich in wildlife. Cyclists might spot various rupicolous birds of prey, such as griffon vultures, golden eagles, and falcons, soaring above the dramatic geological formations. River ecosystems along the Cidacos also support otters and European mink, though these are more elusive.

Q: Are there any options for more advanced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40017714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and thermal baths – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering over 56 km with substantial climbing, providing a rewarding experience for advanced riders.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the no traffic routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquility of the routes, the scenic beauty of the Cidacos Greenway, and the opportunity to combine cycling with visits to natural attractions like the thermal baths of Arnedillo.

Q: Are there any unique geological features to look out for?
A: Yes, the region is known for its dramatic geological profile, shaped by centuries of water and wind erosion. You'll cycle through landscapes that are part of the northern foothills of the Iberian System, offering impressive rock formations and panoramic views, especially as you gain elevation.
tips: Enciso is in a mountainous area of the Iberian System, on the border with Soria. The Cidacos River crosses it and is known for having 3000 dinosaur footprints.

The population of Turruncún reached about 300 inhabitants at the beginning of the 20th century. A large part of these Turruncunese were engaged in mining. The coal mines that are found throughout the entire area were the economic and social engine of Turruncún. However, when these mines were closed, Turruncún slowly declined.

The city of Arnedo is part of Baja Rioja and is located at the foot of the Iberian System. It is a place where you can not only visit its churches, the monastery of Vico, do wine tourism or visit its cave of the hundred pillars, but also visit the area of shops and local shoe factories.
